channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Here are 20-plus kid tools for higher-quality screen time
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Not everything creative needs a prompt. The web is increasingly flooded with AI-generated images and videos, much of it aimed at kids.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: About 4 in 10 worried about environmental impacts of AI: Survey
item.description: A new poll shows growing concerns among citizens over the potential environmental effects caused by artificial intelligence. The Associated Press-NORC Center poll, released Thursday, found about 4 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Scientists spot skyscraper-sized asteroid racing through solar system
item.description: Astronomers have discovered asteroid 2025 SC79, a skyscraper-sized space rock orbiting the sun in just 128 days. the second-fastest known.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The new MacBook Pro: Still a workhorse
item.description: Hello again, and thank you, as always, for spending time with Fast Company’s Plugged In. Apple is legendary for figuring out what people want before they realize they want it. But since 2021, its MacBook Pro hasn’t been like that at all.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Apple may turn off key privacy tool in Europe
item.description: In a victory for surveillance capitalism, Apple may be forced to leave its users in Europe vulnerable to rapacious ad data collection in response to “intense lobbying” by politicians in the region.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Bay Area donors that are helping pay for $300 million White House ballroom
item.description: Trump has said the ballroom will be paid for by himself and donors, with no taxpayer money being used.
item.dc:creator: Bay Area News Group, The Mercury News

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Krafton shifting to 'AI-first' game development
item.description: The makers of popular video game "PUBG: Battlegrounds" are switching gears and becoming an "AI-first" developer.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Parents claim OpenAI weakened chatbot anti-suicide protections just before teen’s death
item.description: OpenAI's launch of its pioneering ChatGPT sparked a global AI craze that has sparked hundreds of billions of dollars in investments in Silicon Valley technology companies, and raised concerns it will be used for harms ranging from rampant unemployment to terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Idaho National Lab testing AI to relieve permitting burdens
item.description: Chris Ritter, the division director for scientific computing and AI at the Idaho National Lab, said using AI to improve basic workflow processes is step one. The post Idaho National Lab testing AI to relieve permitting burdens first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Hidden debug code returns from the dead as TP-Link routers face a wave of new critical root access flaws
item.description: TP-Link vulnerabilities reveal recurring coding issues and incomplete patches which could gift attackers full control of affected routers.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: From friendly text to financial trap: the new scam trend
item.description: Friendly text conversations about BBQs and social events can lead to WEEX gold trading scams that target older adults with fake investment opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump calls off plans to deploy federal troops to San Francisco
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he would not deploy federal troops to San Francisco, reversing course on plans to implement anticrime and immigration enforcement in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: Netflix shakes up Stranger Things season 5 finale release with simultaneous theater and streaming debut in world first
item.description: Netflix isn’t letting the Stranger Things season 5 finale just shout into the streaming void as it confirms the episode will also come to movie theaters.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Schwanke, TechRadar

item.title: How to use keyed services in ASP.NET Core
item.description: Dependency injection (also known as DI) is a design pattern in which the dependent types of a class are injected (passed to it by another class or object) rather than created directly, thereby facilitating loose coupling and promoting easier testing and maintenance. In ASP.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PyTorch team unveils framework for programming clusters
item.description: The PyTorch team at Meta, stewards of the PyTorch open source machine learning framework, has unveiled Monarch, a distributed programming framework intended to bring the simplicity of PyTorch to entire clusters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Iraq bans Roblox over child safety concerns
item.description: Iraq has joined several countries — including China, Turkey, Oman and Qatar — to ban U.S. user-generated video game platform Roblox over child safety.
item.dc:creator: Cher Hwang, NewsNation

item.title: 85% of developers use AI regularly – JetBrains survey
item.description: AI usage has become standard practice in software development, with 85% of developers in a recent JetBrains survey citing regular use of AI tools for coding and development. Additionally, 62% were relying on at least one AI-powered coding assistant, agent, or code editor.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit sues AI company Perplexity and others for 'industrial-scale' scraping of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Modernization with purpose: How federal health agencies can align tech with mission
item.description: Aligning tech, data and workforce to improve health outcomes starts with asking why, Maximus’ Monica Rosser says. The post Modernization with purpose: How federal health agencies can align tech with mission first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Prince Harry, Meghan join call for AI 'superintelligence' ban
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, are joining a call to halt the development of some AI tools until the process can be "done safely and controllably.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Scammers target retirees with election tricks and fake polling updates ahead of Nov 4 vote
item.description: Public voter records expose retirees' personal details to election scammers who create targeted cons using names, addresses, and voting history data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel’s Panther Lake chip could allow Windows 11 to morph into an agentic OS
item.description: Intel’s upcoming “Panther Lake” processor for AI PCs could be a key building block in the transition of Windows 11 into an AI agent-driven OS, analysts said this week. Panther Lake will be available in PCs starting early in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DCIM explained: How data center information management powers AI, capacity planning, and sustainability
item.description: Data center infrastructure management (DCIM) involves managing all aspects of the physical data center. DCIM converges IT management, operations management, facilities management, and automation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AWS DNS error hits DynamoDB, causing problems for multiple services and customers
item.description: Monday got off to a bad start for Amazon Web Services users served by the company’s US-EAST-1 region, when a DNS problem rendered the DynamoDB API unreliable, with consequences for many AWS services and customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World
